questions.text: The ratio of diameters of two wires of the same material is n : 1. The length of each wire is 4 m. On applying the same load, the increase in length of the thin wire will be (n > 1):
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Let's denote the diameter of the thicker wire as D and the diameter of the thinner wire as d. Since the ratio of diameters is n:1, we can write: D/d = n Now, the cross-sectional area of a wire is proportional to the square of its diameter (assuming the wires have the same shape): Area ∝ (diameter)2 So, the cross-sectional area of the thicker wire and the thinner wire are related as follows: A D / A d = (D2) / (d2) = (n2) When the same load is applied to both wires, they experience the same force. The tensile stress is defined as the applied force (F) divided by the cross-sectional area (A) of the wire: Stress = F / A Since the applied force is the same for both wires, we can write Stress D / Stress d = (F / A D) / (F / A d) = A d / A D = 1 / (n2) Now, the strain of a wire is directly proportional to the stress applied to it. So, the strain in the thinner wire and the strain in the thicker wire is related as follows: strain d / strain D = 1 / (n2) Now, the question asks for the increase in length of the thin wire compared to the thick wire. The increase in length is directly proportional to the strain: increase in length d / increase in length D = strain d / strain D = 1 / (n2) Since we know that the increase in length of the thicker wire is the same for both wires and is equal to 4m, we can write: increase in length d = (1 / (n2)) x increase in length D = (1 / (n2)) x 4m = 4m / n2 Therefore, the increase in length of the thin wire is n2 times smaller than the increase in length of the thick wire.

questions.text: A 5 kg mass bounces in simple harmonic motion at the end of a spring. At which point is the acceleration of the mass the greatest?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: When the spring is fully compressed and when the spring is fully extended
options.explanation: When the spring is fully compressed and when the spring is fully extended: When the mass is at the fully compressed position, it experiences maximum acceleration because the spring force is at its maximum, pulling the mass towards the equilibrium position. Similarly, at the fully extended position, the mass also experiences maximum acceleration due to the spring force pulling it back toward the equilibrium position.

options.text: When the spring is at its rest length
options.explanation: When the mass is at the rest length (equilibrium position), it experiences zero acceleration. At this point, the spring force is balanced by the inertial force of the mass, resulting in no net force and, therefore, no acceleration.

options.text: When the spring is halfway between its rest length and is fully extended or compressed length
options.explanation: The acceleration at this point is less than at the fully extended or compressed positions but greater than at the equilibrium position. The acceleration increases as the mass moves away from the equilibrium position (in either direction), reaching its maximum at the fully extended or compressed positions.
This option is incorrect. In simple harmonic motion, the acceleration is not constant but varies with the displacement from the equilibrium position, as described above.

questions.explanation: When the spring is fully compressed or fully extended when Hook's law forces are the greatest, and this indicates that the acceleration is the greatest. F = ma. Option A is correct because the acceleration is greatest when the spring is fully compressed and when it is fully extended. At the rest length, the acceleration is zero, and at the halfway point between rest and fully extended/compressed positions, the acceleration is less than the maximum value but not constant.

questions.text: A tachometer is a device to measure
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. The gravitational pull of an object is measured by its gravitational force, which is the force of attraction between two objects due to their masses and the distance between them. The force of gravity is directly proportional to the product of the masses of the two objects and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them. The gravitational force can be measured using a device called a gravimeter, which measures the acceleration due to gravity. The most common type of gravimeter is the free-fall gravimeter, which measures the time it takes for an object to fall under the influence of gravity.

Incorrect. Surface tension is measured by a device called a tensiometer or surface tensiometer. A tensiometer works by measuring the force required to pull a liquid apart at the surface. The device consists of a small probe or plate that is inserted into the liquid and then slowly pulled out. As the probe is pulled out, it creates a small deformation in the surface of the liquid, which is known as a meniscus. The force required to create this meniscus is measured by the tensiometer, and this force is directly proportional to the surface tension of the liquid. The surface tension is typically measured in units of force per unit length, such as newtons per meter (N/m) or dynes per centimeter (dyn/cm). The surface tension of a liquid can be affected by a variety of factors, such as temperature, pressure, and the presence of dissolved substances.

Correct. A tachometer is a device used to measure the rotational speed of a shaft or disk in a motor or other machine. It measures the number of revolutions per minute (RPM) of the shaft or disk. Tachometers are commonly used in automobiles to measure the RPM of the engine crankshaft, and in aviation to measure the RPM of aircraft engines. They are also used in other industrial and scientific applications where it is important to know the speed of rotation of a machine or component.

Incorrect. The tension in a spring can be measured using a spring balance, also known as a spring scale. A spring balance is a device that uses the extension or compression of a spring to measure force. The spring balance works on the principle of Hooke's law, which states that the force exerted by a spring is directly proportional to its extension or compression. When a force is applied to the spring balance, the spring stretches or compresses, and the amount of deformation of the spring is proportional to the force applied. The scale on the spring balance is calibrated to give a reading of the force in units of weight, such as pounds or kilograms. Therefore, the tension in a spring can be measured by using a spring balance to measure the force required to stretch or compress the spring.

questions.explanation: A tachometer is an instrument that measures the working speed of an engine, typically in revolutions per minute (RPM). It is commonly used in cars, boats, planes, and other vehicles. Most tachometer gauges have either an analog (dial) or digital (LCD or LED screen) display

questions.text: What is wrong about centripetal and centrifugal forces?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: They act on the same body
options.explanation: Yes, centripetal and centrifugal force can act on the same body. However, they are not the same thing. The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is always directed towards the center of the circle. The centrifugal force is an apparent force that acts outward on an object moving in a circular path. It is not a real force, but rather a result of the inertia of the object.

No, the centripetal and centrifugal forces do not both perform work.Centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is directed towards the center of the circle. The centripetal force is necessary to overcome the object's inertia, which wants to keep it moving in a straight line. The centripetal force is always perpendicular to the object's velocity.Centrifugal force is an apparent force that acts on an object moving in a circular path. It is directed outward, away from the center of the circle. The centrifugal force is not a real force, but rather a consequence of inertia. The centrifugal force is equal in magnitude to the centripetal force, but it is in the opposite direction.

Yes, the centripetal and centrifugal forces have opposite directions.The centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is directed towards the center of the circle. The centrifugal force is the apparent outward force that an object feels when it is moving in a circular path. It is directed away from the center of the circle. The centripetal force is provided by a real force, such as the tension in a string or the force of gravity. The centrifugal force is a fictitious force that is not actually present. It is only felt by the object because of its inertia.

questions.text: The centripetal acceleration is also called:

options.explanation: Tangential acceleration is the component of acceleration that acts tangent to the circular path at any given point. It is responsible for changes in the magnitude of the object's linear speed but not for keeping the object on a circular path. Tangential acceleration and centripetal acceleration are related, but they are not the same.

Angular acceleration is the rate of change of angular velocity. It is associated with rotational motion rather than circular motion along a fixed path. While angular acceleration and centripetal acceleration are related when considering rotational motion, they are not synonymous.

Radial acceleration is another term for centripetal acceleration. It is called "radial" because it acts along the radius of the circular path, pointing towards the centre. Radial acceleration is the correct term used to describe the acceleration required to keep an object moving in a circular path.

Rotational acceleration typically refers to the acceleration of an object as it rotates about its axis. It is not the same as centripetal acceleration, which is associated with a circular motion around a centre point.

questions.text: For a body moving with constant speed in a horizontal circle, which of the following remains constant?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Velocity is a vector quantity that includes both magnitude and direction. In circular motion, the direction of velocity is continuously changing because the object is moving in a circular path. Therefore, the velocity is not constant.

Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity and is also a vector quantity. In a circular motion, the direction of acceleration is always directed towards the centre of the circle and is called centripetal acceleration. The magnitude of centripetal acceleration can be constant if the speed is constant, but its direction is continuously changing. Therefore, acceleration is not constant.

Centripetal force is the force that acts towards the centre of the circular path and keeps the object moving in that path. In uniform circular motion (constant speed), the magnitude of the centripetal force remains constant. However, its direction always points towards the centre of the circle, so it is not constant in the sense that it is changing its direction.

Kinetic energy is the energy of motion and is given by the formula KE = (1/2)mv2, where m is the mass of the object, and v is its speed. In this case, the body is moving with a constant speed, and both the mass and speed are constant. Therefore, the kinetic energy remains constant throughout the motion.

questions.text: The angular velocity of the minute hand of a clock is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The angular velocity of the minute hand of a clock is the rate at which it rotates around the center of the clock. It can be calculated by dividing the angle covered by the minute hand in a given time period by that time period. In a standard 12-hour analog clock, the minute hand completes a full revolution (360 degrees) in 60 minutes. options.explanation: The time dilation effect from special relativity is given by the equation:T’= T/ √1-v2/c2Where:T’ is time observed in the moving frame.T is time measured in the stationary frame.v is relative velocity between the frames.c is the speed of light.

questions.text: In case of a vibrating pendulum the potential energy is maximum at
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The potential energy of simple pendulum is given by:U=mghWhere:m= massg= gravitational accelerationh= heightIn the context of a vibrating (oscillating) pendulum, the potential energy is related to the displacement from the equilibrium position. At the extreme position (maximum displacement from the equilibrium), the height h above the reference point is maximized. This results in the maximum potential energy for the pendulum
